message:
fix(h2): normalize stream-0 error construction to connection-level error

HTTP/2 stream ID 0 is reserved for connection-level signaling and must
never appear as a stream-level error target. If H2Error::stream() was
called with stream_id=0 (likely from a code path that extracted a stream
ID from a malformed frame), it would create a stream-level error tagged
with stream 0, confusing downstream error classification — handlers that
check is_connection_error() would see false, but the error actually
pertains to the connection.

Now H2Error::stream(0, ...) detects this case and delegates to
H2Error::connection(), ensuring the resulting error has stream_id=None,
is_connection_error() returns true, and the Display output correctly
reports "connection error".

Adds a test verifying the normalization: stream(0, ...) produces a
connection-level error with no stream_id and correct display text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
